titleStmt.title: Letter from Lord Courtown to the Chief Secretary’s Office, 21 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: James Walter Milles Stopford

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from James Walter Milles Stopford (1853–1933), 6th Earl of Courtown, County Wexford, to the Chief Secretary's Office, forwarding the names of his two nominees for the position of Deputy Lieutenant for County Wexford as replacements for the previous incumbents (deceased) and asks the Chief Secretary's Office to request the approval of the Lords Justices.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/5620/12549

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
COURTOWN HOUSE, GOREY, IRELAND
Station, Gorey: Telegrams, Courtown Harbour. July 21st 1916 Sir, I should be obliged if you would obtain
the consent of the Lords Justices to my appointing
the Hon bleArthur Claud Spencer Chichester of
Dunbrody Park, Arthurstown and Sit George
Errington Bart of Ramsfort, Gorey, Deputy
Lieutnt for the County Wexford in the place of
Mr R Donovan and Mr F R Leigh both
deceased Yrs obediently Courtown H.M.L. Co Wexford
